When we do programmatic SEO, a lot of times we will create a template for a page, populate that template with either generated or human interaction content, and then programmatically publish those pages, either using GitHub or whatever apparatus to commit them and then publish them, or even logging onto their FTP server and just publishing the pages, whatever the interface they have that we need to use.

Because the big problem with this kind of org is that we do an enormous amount of work on the content internally, but then the execution is just, by execution, I mean publishing in the case of content, is so mission critical because it can undermine the work that we put dozens or hundreds of hours into.

So, it becomes a pretty big deal for us to execute well.

way we operate with new clients is that we have a process for onboarding new clients that basically puts them through a sprint.

From them on everything that we need to know in order to create the proper content for them, whether that's the context of their company, what their products are, who their ICB is, you know, their clients and customers, and then to understand the voice and tone that they want to go after.
This helps us create our initial artifacts for our pipelines that are company context, audience and tone, which is, you know, audience and personas, and then voice and tone.

We have now publishing content.

We've got an articulation of what they want, how the content is being produced, the pipelines have been created to produce that content, and now we're starting to publish for them.
